metadata.title: Road to Self-Government
metadata.summary: A sequencing and significance activity about major constitutional and political developments in post-war Singapore.
metadata.subject: humanities
metadata.learningObjective: Sequence key developments in Singapore's path to internal self-government and connect each event to its historical significance.

components.text: Arrange the developments by date, then match each one to the change it represents. Dates help with order; significance explains why the sequence matters.

components.prompt: Place the developments in chronological order, earliest first.

content.text: 1957: Lim Yew Hock leads successful constitutional talks
1948: Singapore holds its first limited Legislative Council election
1955: The Rendel Constitution expands elected representation

feedback.correct: Correct. The sequence shows political participation and local responsibility expanding over time.
feedback.incorrect: Use the dates first, then check that the constitutional changes build towards full internal self-government.
feedback.explanation: Limited elections in 1948 were followed by wider representation in 1955, successful talks in 1957 and internal self-government in 1959.

components.prompt: Match each development with its main significance.

content.text: Created a legislature with a majority of elected seats
Secured agreement for greater local control
Placed most domestic affairs under an elected local government

feedback.correct: All matched. You have connected chronology to political significance.
feedback.incorrect: Distinguish taking part in elections, expanding elected seats, negotiating change and exercising local control.
feedback.explanation: Each development widened either representation or the responsibilities exercised by locally elected leaders.
